# RustWerk

> Git-native, AI-agent-friendly project orchestration
> CLI. Single binary, zero config, JSON-native.

RustWerk manages tasks, dependencies, effort tracking,
and schedule visualization from the command line. All
project state lives in `.rustwerk/project.json` —
committed alongside your code.

## Quickstart

```
rustwerk init "My Project"
rustwerk task add "Task title" --id TASK-ID \
  --complexity 5 --effort 2D
rustwerk task list --available
rustwerk task status TASK-ID in-progress
rustwerk task status TASK-ID done
```

## Value Constraints

### Task IDs

Pattern: `[A-Za-z0-9_-]+` (non-empty, stored
uppercase). Auto-generated if omitted: T0001, T0002,
etc. Use mnemonic prefixes by area (e.g. DOM-, CLI-).

### Developer IDs

Pattern: `[a-z0-9_-]+` (non-empty, stored lowercase).

### Status Values

`todo`, `in-progress`, `blocked`, `done`, `on-hold`.
Parser also accepts underscore and concatenated forms
(e.g. `in_progress`, `inprogress`).

Valid transitions (without `--force`):

```
todo        → in-progress, on-hold
in-progress → done, blocked
blocked     → in-progress, todo
on-hold     → todo, in-progress
done        → (terminal)
```

### Effort Format

Grammar: `<positive-number><unit>` where unit is
`H` (hours), `D` (days=8H), `W` (weeks=40H),
`M` (months=160H). Case-insensitive. Fractional
values allowed. Examples: `5H`, `2.5D`, `0.5W`, `1M`.

### Tags

Pattern per tag: `[a-z0-9-]+`, max 50 chars. Max 20
tags per task. Input is trimmed, lowercased, sorted,
and deduplicated.

### Complexity

Integer, range 1–1000.

## CLI Commands

### Task Management

```
rustwerk task add "Title" [--id ID] [--desc DESC] \
  [--complexity N] [--effort AMT] [--tags TAGS]
rustwerk task update <ID> [--title T] [--desc D] \
  [--tags T]
rustwerk task remove <ID>
rustwerk task rename <OLD_ID> <NEW_ID>
rustwerk task status <ID> <STATUS> [--force]
rustwerk task list [--available] [--active] \
  [--status S] [--assignee DEV] [--chain ID] \
  [--tag TAG]
rustwerk task assign <ID> [DEV_ID]
rustwerk task unassign <ID>
rustwerk task depend <FROM> <TO>
rustwerk task undepend <FROM> <TO>
rustwerk task describe <ID>
```

### Effort Tracking

```
rustwerk effort estimate <ID> <AMOUNT>
rustwerk effort log <ID> <AMOUNT> [--dev DEV] \
  [--note NOTE]
```

Task must be in-progress to log effort.

### Developer Management

```
rustwerk dev add <ID> <NAME> [--email E] [--role R]
rustwerk dev remove <ID>
rustwerk dev list
```

### Visualization and Reports

```
rustwerk show                  # project summary
rustwerk status                # compact dashboard
rustwerk tree [--remaining]    # dependency tree
rustwerk gantt [--remaining]   # ASCII Gantt chart
rustwerk report complete       # completion summary
rustwerk report effort         # effort by developer
rustwerk report bottlenecks    # blocking tasks
```

## Batch Commands (Atomic JSON)

Pipe a JSON array to `rustwerk batch`. All commands
execute atomically — all succeed or none are applied.
Environment variable defaults (e.g. `RUSTWERK_USER`)
do NOT apply inside batch; all values must be explicit.

Limits: 10 MB input, 1000 commands per batch.

### Schema

```json
[
  {
    "command": "<command-name>",
    "args": { ... }
  }
]
```

### Command Reference

#### task.add

Create a new task.

- **Required:** `title` (string)
- **Optional:** `id` (string), `desc` (string),
  `complexity` (integer), `effort` (string),
  `tags` (array of strings)

```json
{"command": "task.add", "args": {
  "title": "Implement auth",
  "id": "AUTH-01",
  "complexity": 5,
  "effort": "2D",
  "tags": ["backend", "security"]
}}
```

#### task.remove

- **Required:** `id` (string)

```json
{"command": "task.remove", "args": {"id": "AUTH-01"}}
```

#### task.update

Update an existing task. At least one optional field
must be provided.

- **Required:** `id` (string)
- **Optional:** `title` (string), `desc` (string;
  empty string clears description),
  `tags` (array of strings)

```json
{"command": "task.update", "args": {
  "id": "AUTH-01",
  "title": "Implement OAuth2",
  "tags": ["backend", "security", "oauth"]
}}
```

#### task.rename

Change a task's ID. Updates all dependency references
across other tasks. The task's status, effort log,
tags, and assignee are preserved. The per-task
description file at `.rustwerk/tasks/<ID>.md` is moved
by the CLI batch driver after the JSON is persisted.
Task ID references in free-form text are not rewritten.

- **Required:** `old_id` (string), `new_id` (string)

```json
{"command": "task.rename", "args": {
  "old_id": "AUTH-01", "new_id": "AUTH-SIGNIN"
}}
```

#### task.status

- **Required:** `id` (string), `status` (string)
- **Optional:** `force` (boolean, default false)

```json
{"command": "task.status", "args": {
  "id": "AUTH-01", "status": "in-progress"
}}
```

#### task.assign

- **Required:** `id` (string), `to` (string —
  must be a registered developer ID)

```json
{"command": "task.assign", "args": {
  "id": "AUTH-01", "to": "alice"
}}
```

#### task.unassign

- **Required:** `id` (string)

```json
{"command": "task.unassign", "args": {"id": "AUTH-01"}}
```

#### task.depend

Declare that `from` depends on `to` (i.e. `to` must
finish before `from` can start). No cycles allowed.

- **Required:** `from` (string), `to` (string)

```json
{"command": "task.depend", "args": {
  "from": "AUTH-02", "to": "AUTH-01"
}}
```

#### task.undepend

- **Required:** `from` (string), `to` (string)

```json
{"command": "task.undepend", "args": {
  "from": "AUTH-02", "to": "AUTH-01"
}}
```

#### effort.estimate

- **Required:** `id` (string), `amount` (string)

```json
{"command": "effort.estimate", "args": {
  "id": "AUTH-01", "amount": "3D"
}}
```

#### effort.log

- **Required:** `id` (string), `amount` (string),
  `dev` (string — developer ID)
- **Optional:** `note` (string)

```json
{"command": "effort.log", "args": {
  "id": "AUTH-01", "amount": "4H",
  "dev": "alice", "note": "JWT integration"
}}
```

#### dev.add

- **Required:** `id` (string), `name` (string)
- **Optional:** `email` (string), `role` (string)

```json
{"command": "dev.add", "args": {
  "id": "alice", "name": "Alice Chen",
  "email": "alice@example.com", "role": "backend"
}}
```

#### dev.remove

- **Required:** `id` (string)

```json
{"command": "dev.remove", "args": {"id": "alice"}}
```

### Batch Output

On success, stdout returns a JSON array:

```json
[
  {"index": 0, "ok": true, "message": "..."},
  {"index": 1, "ok": true, "message": "..."}
]
```

On failure, stderr returns:

```json
{"error": "...", "applied": 0}
```

No commands are applied on failure.

### Common Workflows

**Create tasks with dependencies:**

```json
[
  {"command": "task.add", "args": {
    "title": "Design schema", "id": "DB-01",
    "complexity": 3, "effort": "1D",
    "tags": ["database"]
  }},
  {"command": "task.add", "args": {
    "title": "Implement models", "id": "DB-02",
    "complexity": 5, "effort": "2D",
    "tags": ["database"]
  }},
  {"command": "task.add", "args": {
    "title": "Write migrations", "id": "DB-03",
    "complexity": 3, "effort": "1D",
    "tags": ["database"]
  }},
  {"command": "task.depend", "args": {
    "from": "DB-02", "to": "DB-01"
  }},
  {"command": "task.depend", "args": {
    "from": "DB-03", "to": "DB-02"
  }}
]
```

**Set up a team and assign work:**

```json
[
  {"command": "dev.add", "args": {
    "id": "alice", "name": "Alice Chen",
    "role": "backend"
  }},
  {"command": "dev.add", "args": {
    "id": "bob", "name": "Bob Park",
    "role": "frontend"
  }},
  {"command": "task.assign", "args": {
    "id": "DB-01", "to": "alice"
  }},
  {"command": "task.assign", "args": {
    "id": "UI-01", "to": "bob"
  }}
]
```

**Progress a task through its lifecycle:**

```json
[
  {"command": "task.status", "args": {
    "id": "DB-01", "status": "in-progress"
  }},
  {"command": "effort.log", "args": {
    "id": "DB-01", "amount": "4H",
    "dev": "alice", "note": "initial schema draft"
  }},
  {"command": "effort.log", "args": {
    "id": "DB-01", "amount": "2H",
    "dev": "alice", "note": "review feedback"
  }},
  {"command": "task.status", "args": {
    "id": "DB-01", "status": "done"
  }}
]
```

## Core Concepts

- **Dependencies** form a DAG (cycles rejected).
  "B depends on A" means A must finish before B starts
- **Critical path**: longest chain of remaining work,
  marked with `*` in listings
- **Batch commands**: atomic JSON operations via stdin
  — all succeed or none are applied

## Environment Variables

- `RUSTWERK_USER`: default developer ID for assign
  and effort log commands (CLI only, not batch)

## Project Detection

RustWerk looks for `.rustwerk/` starting from the
current directory and walking up, similar to git.

## Optional: docs

- [User Manual](docs/manual.md): complete reference
  with examples
- [Project File Spec](docs/project-file-spec.md):
  JSON schema for .rustwerk/project.json
